name: Chicken Phyllo Wraps
recipeIngredient: 3 cups cooked rice (cooked without salt and fat)
10 oz pk chopped spinach, thawed and well drained
1 can 2 1/4 oz sliced black olives, drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Coat a large skillet with cooking spray and heat over medium-high heat until hot.
2. Add the chicken, mushrooms, and onion; cook and stir 2 to 4 minutes or until the chicken is no longer pink and the vegetables are tender.
3. Reduce the heat to medium. Add the rice, ricotta cheese, spinach, olives, nuts, garlic, oregano, and lemon pepper; cook and stir 3 to 4 minutes until well blended and thoroughly heated.
4. Working with one phyllo sheet at a time, spray with cooking spray and fold the sheet in half lengthwise.
5. Place 3/4 to 1 cup rice mixture on one end of the phyllo strip.
6. Fold the left bottom corner over the mixture, forming a triangle. Continue folding back and forth into a triangle until the end of the strip is reached.
7. Repeat with the remaining phyllo sheets and rice mixture.
8. Place the triangles, seam side down, on a baking sheet coated with cooking spray.
9. Coat the top of each triangle with cooking spray.
10. Bake at 400°F for 15 to 20 minutes or until golden brown.
Note: To toast nuts, place them on a baking sheet and bake at 350°F for 5 to 7 minutes or until lightly browned.
